facility_name: El Capitan — Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL)
full_address: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Livermore, CA
cooling_type: 100% fanless direct liquid cooling (CoolIT Split-Flow cold plate technology)
opportunity_summary: World's fastest supercomputer (#1 TOP500, Nov 2024). 1.742 exaflops. 100% fanless DLC by CoolIT Systems using patented Split-Flow cold plates across 11,000+ compute nodes. 43,808 AMD EPYC Genoa CPUs + 43,808 AMD Instinct MI300A GPUs. ~30 MW peak power. HPE Cray EX platform.
confidence_reason: HPE official press release, CoolIT press release, and HPCwire reporting all confirm 100% DLC, AMD MI300A GPUs, ~30MW power. #1 on TOP500 Nov 2024.
source_1_title: HPE delivers world's fastest direct liquid-cooled exascale supercomputer El Capitan for LLNL
source_1_evidence_snippet: The 100% fanless direct liquid-cooled El Capitan is also one of the top 20 most energy-efficient systems in the world. El Capitan runs at 1.742 exaflops.
source_2_title: Direct Liquid Cooling for El Capitan — CoolIT Systems
source_2_evidence_snippet: CoolIT's patented Split-Flow technology is embedded in the single-phase, direct liquid cooling loops that provide efficient cooling to more than 11,000 compute nodes.
notes: World's #1 supercomputer. CoolIT DLC is the cooling vendor. System decommissions predecessor Sierra. 58.89 GFlops/watt energy efficiency.

full_address: Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Oak Ridge, TN
customer_type_if_known: U.S. Department of Energy / Office of Science
cooling_type: warm water direct liquid cooling (85F/29.4C non-prechilled water, 4x 350HP pumps, 6000 gal/min)
opportunity_summary: World's first exascale supercomputer (2022). 1.206 exaflops HPL. HPE Cray EX platform, warm-water DLC at 85F. 4x 350HP pumps circulate 6,000 gal/min. AMD EPYC 64C CPUs + AMD Instinct MI250X GPUs. ~29MW IT load, 40MW facility total. 40MW cooling capacity expandable to 70MW.
confidence_reason: HPE official press release, OLCF official page, Nature scientific data paper, and multiple DOE sources confirm warm-water DLC, 29MW IT power, 40MW facility.
source_1_title: HPE ushers in new era with world's first exascale supercomputer Frontier
source_1_evidence_snippet: Sophisticated liquid-cooling capabilities that efficiently remove heat from high power devices through an auxiliary Cooling Distribution Unit (CDU).
source_2_title: Energy dataset of Frontier supercomputer for waste heat recovery
source_2_evidence_snippet: Frontier consumes between 8 and 30 MW of electricity depending on operational load.
notes: First exascale system globally (2022). 4x 350HP pumps flow 6,000 gal/min of 85F water. 30-40% more cooling efficiency vs predecessor Summit. Facility cooling expandable to 70MW. Waste heat recovery studied (Nature paper).
heat_recovery_details: Waste heat recovery studied per Nature Scientific Data paper (2024). Warm water at 85F enables potential heat reuse.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Operational since May 2022. TOP500 #2 as of Nov 2024.

customer_type_if_known: U.S. Department of Energy / Office of Science
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (44,000 gal water loop with cooling towers, chillers, heat exchangers, filtration)
opportunity_summary: Intel-based exascale supercomputer. 2+ exaflops peak. HPE Cray EX platform with DLC. 63,744 Intel Data Center Max (Ponte Vecchio) GPUs + 21,248 Intel Xeon Max CPUs. 44,000 gal water cooling loop. ~39MW power consumption.
confidence_reason: Argonne ALCF official page, DOE announcement, multiple industry sources confirm DLC, Intel GPU configuration, and ~39MW power.
source_1_title: Aurora | Argonne Leadership Computing Facility
source_1_evidence_snippet: 63,744 compute GPUs designed to achieve performance of over 2 FP64 ExaFLOPS.
source_2_title: Argonne lays the groundwork for its next-generation supercomputer
source_2_evidence_snippet: Argonne had to upgrade its cooling capacity to pump 44,000 gallons of water through a complex loop of pipes.
notes: Intel-based exascale system. Only TOP500 exascale system using Intel GPUs (Ponte Vecchio). 44,000 gal water cooling loop with towers, chillers, heat exchangers. ~39MW power.
gpu_model: Intel Data Center GPU Max (Ponte Vecchio)

full_address: Los Alamos National Laboratory, Los Alamos, NM
cooling_type: 100% direct liquid cooling (HPE Cray EX)
opportunity_summary: LANL's AI supercomputer with 2,560 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips + 920 Grace CPU Superchips. All liquid-cooled HPE Cray EX platform. 10 exaflops peak AI performance. First large-scale NVIDIA Grace CPU deployment in the US. TOP500 #19.
confidence_reason: DOE official press release, NVIDIA blog, HPCwire, and DCD all confirm all-liquid-cooled design with GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips.
source_1_title: NNSA's LANL launches frontier AI models on the Venado supercomputer
source_1_evidence_snippet: Venado, which uses NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips, is the 19th-fastest supercomputer in the world.
source_2_title: Venado: The AI Supercomputer Built to Tackle Science's Biggest Challenges
source_2_evidence_snippet: Venado was built with 2,560 direct, liquid-cooled NVIDIA Grace Hopper Superchips in an exascale-class HPE Cray EX supercomputer.
notes: First large-scale deployment of NVIDIA Grace CPU Superchips in the US. 10 exaflops peak AI performance. TOP500 #19.

facility_name: Perlmutter — NERSC, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
operator_company: National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC)
parent_company: U.S. Department of Energy / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
full_address: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Berkeley, CA
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling with free-cooling (water cooling towers, no mechanical chillers)
opportunity_summary: HPE Cray EX supercomputer with 6,144 NVIDIA A100 GPUs across 1,792 GPU nodes + 3,072 CPU-only nodes. DLC with free cooling (no chillers). PUE 1.05-1.08. Named after Nobel laureate Saul Perlmutter.
confidence_reason: NERSC official documentation, Berkeley Lab announcement, and HPE specifications confirm DLC, A100 GPUs, and PUE 1.05-1.08.
source_1_title: Perlmutter System — NERSC
source_1_evidence_snippet: The system features NVIDIA A100 GPUs with Tensor Core technology and direct liquid cooling.
source_2_title: Berkeley Lab Deploys Next-Gen Supercomputer Perlmutter
source_2_evidence_snippet: Free cooling technique requires only water cooling towers and no energy-hungry mechanical chillers, bringing PUE down to 1.05-1.08.
notes: PUE 1.05-1.08 achieved through free cooling with no mechanical chillers. HPE Cray Slingshot interconnect.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Fully operational. HPE Cray EX with DLC, PUE 1.05-1.08.

full_address: National Renewable Energy Laboratory, Golden, CO
customer_type_if_known: U.S. DOE / NREL renewable energy research
cooling_type: 100% direct liquid cooling for CPU nodes; warm-water system with heat reuse
opportunity_summary: NREL's flagship HPC. 44 petaflops. PUE 1.03 — among most efficient DCs globally. 100% DLC for CPU nodes. 132 GPU nodes with 4x NVIDIA H100 each + 2,314 CPU nodes. Warm-water heat captured and reused for ESIF building heating and sidewalk de-icing.
confidence_reason: DOE official announcement, NREL official page, Intel customer story, and HPCwire all confirm 100% DLC, PUE 1.03, H100 GPUs, and 44 PFLOPS.
source_1_title: DOE's Newest Energy Research-Dedicated Supercomputer Arrives at NREL
source_1_evidence_snippet: Kestrel supercomputer completed full buildout in August 2024 to reach 44 petaflops.
source_2_title: High-Performance Computing Data Center Warm-Water Liquid Cooling
source_2_evidence_snippet: Warm-water liquid cooling captures 97% of waste heat. Heat reused for office/lab space in ESIF. PUE 1.03.
notes: Successor to Eagle (8 PFLOPS, 2MW, also warm-water DLC). 97% waste heat captured and reused for ESIF building heating and sidewalk de-icing. 10.4 GFlops/watt.
heat_recovery_details: 97% waste heat captured via warm-water system. Used to heat ESIF office and lab spaces, and de-ice sidewalks in winter.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Full buildout completed August 2024. 44 PFLOPS, PUE 1.03.

cooling_type: Chilldyne negative pressure liquid cooling + Penguin Tundra infrastructure
opportunity_summary: Sandia's Vanguard 2 system. Features 128 NextSilicon Maverick-2 dual-die accelerators. Chilldyne negative pressure liquid cooling + Penguin Tundra infrastructure. Explores novel compute architectures for national security. Sandia also tested Submer immersion cooling at their HPC center.
confidence_reason: Sandia official press release and HPCwire confirm Chilldyne liquid cooling and NextSilicon accelerators. Submer immersion testing also confirmed by DCD.
source_1_title: Not the largest supercomputer, but maybe the most interesting
source_1_evidence_snippet: Advanced Chilldyne negative pressure liquid cooling system and Penguin's Tundra infrastructure optimize thermal management.
source_2_title: Sandia High Performance Computing center tests Submer immersion cooling
source_2_evidence_snippet: Sandia announced testing immersion cooling at its HPC center, estimating it will reduce energy consumption by as much as 70 percent.
notes: Sandia actively evaluating both DLC (Chilldyne) and immersion (Submer) for HPC. Novel non-GPU accelerator architecture (NextSilicon Maverick-2). Submer immersion testing estimates 70% energy reduction.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Operational 2025. Vanguard 2 program with Chilldyne DLC.

cooling_type: Eviden BullSequana XH3000 direct liquid cooling with warm water; waste heat piped to campus heating
opportunity_summary: Europe's first exascale supercomputer. 1 ExaFLOP/s. #4 TOP500. Most energy-efficient exascale system globally (60 GFlops/watt). #1 Green500 for exascale class. ~24,000 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. Eviden BullSequana XH3000 DLC. ~17MW power budget. Waste heat heats campus buildings. EUR 500M total budget.
confidence_reason: EuroHPC JU official page, Forschungszentrum Juelich press releases, NVIDIA newsroom all confirm BullSequana XH3000 DLC, ~24K GH200, 1 ExaFLOP, 17MW, waste heat reuse.
source_1_title: Europe's First Supercomputer Reaches 1 ExaFLOP/s
source_1_evidence_snippet: JUPITER has become the first supercomputer in Europe to reach 1 ExaFLOP/s.
source_2_title: JUPITER Officially Propels Europe into the Exascale Era
source_2_evidence_snippet: JUPITER is the most energy efficient exascale system at 60 gigaflops per watt. Warm water cooling with waste heat used to heat campus buildings.
notes: EUR 500M total budget (273M hardware, 227M operations). Europe's first exascale. Waste heat integrated into Juelich campus heating network. #1 Green500 exascale class.
heat_recovery_details: Waste heat from warm-water cooling piped into Juelich campus heating network.

source_1_evidence_snippet: Leonardo absorbs approximately 9 MW of power. With Phase 2, total power will reach approximately 20 MW. PUE estimated below 1.1.
source_2_title: Leonardo HPC System
source_2_evidence_snippet: 4992 liquid-cooled compute nodes. Direct liquid cooling circulating through cold plates extracts 95% of dissipated heat. 10km of pipes, 500m3 water flow.

parent_company: EuroHPC Joint Undertaking / LUMI Consortium (10 countries)
cooling_type: liquid circulation cooling with 100% free cooling; waste heat to district heating
opportunity_summary: 380 PFLOPS. HPE Cray EX with AMD MI250X GPUs. PUE 1.03 with 100% free cooling. 100% hydroelectric power. Waste heat provides 20% of Kajaani city district heating — negative CO2 footprint (13,500 tons/year reduced). Greenest supercomputer in Europe.
confidence_reason: CSC official page, EuroHPC JU, LUMI project site, and DCD all confirm PUE 1.03, 100% free cooling, hydroelectric, district heating (20% of Kajaani).

source_2_evidence_snippet: Waste heat provides about 20 percent of district heating in the city of Kajaani.
notes: Median power 3.8MW, max 5.8MW (LUMI-G partition). Nameplate TDP ~8MW. Negative CO2 footprint due to district heating displacement. 200MW grid feed available. Greenest supercomputer in Europe (#3 Green500 globally).

source_2_evidence_snippet: From a depth of 45 meters, 460 liters of cold lake water per second are supplied via 2.8 km long pipes.

facility_name: Fugaku — RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS), Kobe
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (water at ~15C to CPUs, 9:1 water-to-air ratio, Fujikura cold plates, secondary pipe system)
opportunity_summary: Former world #1 supercomputer. 442 PFLOPS. ~160,000 Fujitsu A64FX ARM CPUs. DLC with 9:1 water-to-air ratio. ~15C inlet water, exits 19-25C. ~30MW power (constrained by facility 30MW capacity). 100+ kW/m2 heat density. Successor announced 2025 with 32C warm water cooling.
confidence_reason: RIKEN official pages, Fujitsu technical review, TOP500 entry, and Staubli case study confirm DLC, 9:1 water ratio, ~30MW, Fujikura cold plates.
source_1_title: Supporting Facilities — RIKEN R-CCS
source_1_evidence_snippet: Water cooling to air cooling ratio of 9:1. Secondary water system directly cools ~160,000 CPUs at ~15C.
source_2_title: System Implementation Technologies of Supercomputer Fugaku
source_2_evidence_snippet: Development process constrained by 30MW capacity of the Center for Computational Science at RIKEN.
notes: Former #1 TOP500 (2020-2022). Fujikura cold plates on CPUs. 100+ kW/m2 heat density. Successor system announced Nov 2025: will use NVIDIA GH200 + Fujitsu Monaka chips with 32C warm water cooling.
expansion_details: Successor announced Nov 2025 with NVIDIA GH200 + Fujitsu Monaka, 32C warm water cooling replacing 15C cold water.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: System configuration finalized for new RIKEN supercomputer. 32C warm water cooling.

facility_name: Sunway TaihuLight — National Supercomputing Center, Wuxi

opportunity_summary: 93 PFLOPS. Former #1 TOP500 (2016-2018). 15.37 MW average power. 28 MW Climaveneta cooling system with 15 watercooled chillers (magnetic levitation VFD compressors). Domestic Sunway SW26010 processors. TOP500 #24 as of Nov 2025.

full_address: Mike and Ophelia Lazaridis Quantum-Nano Centre, University of Waterloo, ON, Canada
cooling_type: single-phase immersion cooling (9 tanks, Hypertec Trident) + direct-to-chip liquid cooling for GPU subsystems
opportunity_summary: Canada's first large-scale immersion-cooled supercomputer. Hypertec Trident immersion-born servers. Intel Xeon 6 Granite Rapids AP CPUs. 9 immersion tanks + DLC for GPUs. 50% cooling energy reduction vs air. 40% smaller footprint. 100% heat reuse to Lazaridis Quantum-Nano Centre.
confidence_reason: Hypertec official press release, DCD feature, and Nokia announcement confirm immersion cooling, Hypertec Trident, 9 tanks, heat reuse, Intel Xeon 6.
source_1_title: Inside Nibi: How immersion cooling and AI-scale compute are changing Canadian HPC
source_1_evidence_snippet: Canada's first large-scale immersion-cooled supercomputer. 9 single-phase immersion cooling tanks. DLC for GPU subsystems. 50% cooling energy reduction.
source_2_title: Nokia and Hypertec Group to Power Nibi Supercomputer at University of Waterloo
source_2_evidence_snippet: 100% heat reuse, with waste heat captured and repurposed to warm the Lazaridis Quantum-Nano Centre.
notes: First large-scale immersion-cooled HPC in Canada. Hypertec Trident immersion-born servers (components submerged in dielectric fluid). Nokia partnership for networking. 4,000+ users nationwide.
heat_recovery_details: 100% heat reuse. Waste heat captured and repurposed to warm the Mike and Ophelia Lazaridis Quantum-Nano Centre at University of Waterloo.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Fully operational. Canada's first large-scale immersion-cooled supercomputer.

facility_name: Blue Lion (planned) — Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ), Garching
parent_company: Bavarian Academy of Sciences / BMBF / Bavarian State Ministry
customer_type_if_known: Gauss Centre for Supercomputing / Bavarian research
cooling_type: 100% direct liquid cooling with 40C warm water (HPE Cray next-gen); waste heat reuse planned
opportunity_summary: EUR 250M next-gen supercomputer. 100% DLC at 40C warm water. HPE Cray with NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture. 30x performance vs SuperMUC-NG. Waste heat will heat offices and potentially neighboring organizations. Gauss Centre for Supercomputing national resource.
confidence_reason: HPE official press release, LRZ official announcement, and multiple industry sources confirm 100% DLC at 40C, NVIDIA Vera Rubin, EUR 250M, 2027 target.
source_1_title: HPE builds next-generation 100% DLC supercomputer at LRZ
source_1_evidence_snippet: Blue Lion is 100% direct liquid cooled with up to 40C warm water, enabling the use of waste heat.
source_2_title: HPE builds next-generation supercomputer at the LRZ
source_2_evidence_snippet: EUR 250M total cost. 30x faster than SuperMUC-NG. NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture. Live early 2027.
notes: Uses NVIDIA Vera Rubin chip architecture (next-gen after Blackwell). 40C warm water enables chillerless waste heat reuse. Will supply waste heat to LRZ offices and potentially neighbor organizations.
heat_recovery_details: 40C warm water DLC enables waste heat reuse. LRZ already uses waste heat from current SuperMUC-NG; Blue Lion will extend to neighboring organizations.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Announced Dec 2024. EUR 250M. 100% DLC at 40C. Live early 2027.
